channel.title: Tv on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Tv on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Sightings
item.description:
Sightings is an American paranormal and news television show that began in the 1990s. Initially airing as a special titled The UFO Report: Sightings in 1991, it evolved into a weekly program investigating topics like UFOs, ghosts, and Bigfoot. The show was presented in an investigative news format and hosted by Tim White. Sightings aired on various networks, including Fox, and later in syndication, before concluding its run in 1998 with special episodes.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sightings_%28TV_series%29

item.title: Sightings (TV series)
item.description:
Sightings was a paranormal-themed television series that aired from 1991 to 1997, featuring an investigative news format hosted by Tim White. The program covered a variety of fringe topics, including UFO sightings, ghosts, and Bigfoot, and was executive produced by Henry Winkler and Ann Daniel. Following its original run on Fox and in syndication, the show’s legacy continued through various specials and the 2002 television movie Sightings: Heartland Ghost. It remains a notable example of 1990s paranormal television that explored controversial subjects like animal mutilations and extraterrestrial encounters.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sightings_%28TV_series%29

item.title: The Unexplained Files
item.description:
The Unexplained Files is an American documentary television series that aired on Science Channel. The series investigated paranormal and unexplained phenomena across two seasons, with the first season having 8 episodes and the second season planning 12. While it was the most watched series on the Science Channel during its first season, critics noted its tendency to focus on sensationalized theories over scientific explanations. One review highlighted how a veterinarian’s explanation for cattle mutilations was given minimal airtime compared to “outlandish theories,” leading to criticism of the show as biased.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Unexplained_Files

item.title: UFO Coverup? Live
item.description:
UFO Coverup? Live was a US television broadcast that aired on October 14, 1988, and introduced the Majestic 12 hoax and Area 51 to Americans. Hosted by Mike Farrell, the program discussed various UFO incidents and featured interviews with ufologists, pilots, and skeptics. It also included clips from films with alien themes and presented claims from informants about aliens and a government agreement for an Area 51 base. The program later faced criticism for its truthfulness and was noted for influencing The X-Files.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_Cover_Up%3F_Live
